quality.rationale: 基于相对论热力学第一定律与理想气体状态方程，结合等熵条件进行多步微分与代数推导

origin.question: Problem 5.32. As in non-relativistic thermodynamics, one defines the specific heat of a gas at constant volume and constant pressure by$$\mathbf {c_{v}} = \mathrm {T} \left. \frac {\mathrm {d s}}{\mathrm {d T}}\right|_{\mathbf {n}} \qquad \mathbf {c_{p}} = \mathrm {T} \left. \frac {\mathrm {d s}}{\mathrm {d T}}\right|_{\mathbf {p}} .$$For a perfect Maxwell-Boltzmann gas, show that $c_{\mathbf{p}} = c_{\mathbf{v}} + \mathbf{k}$ . (Here $\mathbf{k} =$ Boltzmann's constant.) Show that the adiabatic index$$\Gamma_{1} \equiv \frac {\partial \log p}{\partial \log n} |_{s}$$is equal to the ratio of specific heats,$$\gamma \equiv c_{\mathbf {p}} / c_{\mathbf {v}}.$$
origin.solution: Solution 5.32. For a perfect Maxwell-Boltzmann gas,$$p = n k T \tag {1}$$$$\rho / \mathfrak {n} = \mathrm {U} ( \mathrm {T} ) \tag {2}$$Equation (2) says that the energy per particle is a function of temperature alone. From the first law of relativistic thermodynamics,$$\begin{array}{l} \mathrm {T d s} = \mathrm {d} ( \rho / \mathrm {n} ) + \mathrm {p d} ( 1 / \mathrm {n} ) \\ = \frac {\mathrm {d} U} {\mathrm {d} T} \mathrm {d} T + \mathrm {p d} \left( \frac {1} {n}\right) \tag {3} \\ \end{array}$$we see that$$c_{v} = \frac {d U} {d T} . \tag {4}$$Equation (1) implies that$$\mathrm {p d} \left( \frac {1} {\mathrm {n}}\right) + \frac {1} {\mathrm {n}} \mathrm {d p} = \mathrm {k dT} \tag {5}$$so Equation (3) becomes$$\mathrm {T d s} = \left( \frac {\mathrm {d} U} {\mathrm {d} T} + \mathrm {k}\right) \mathrm {d} T - \frac {1} {\mathrm {n}} \mathrm {d p} \tag {6}$$and thus$$c_{p} = \frac {d U} {d T} + k = c_{v} + k . \tag {7}$$From Equation (1)$$\Gamma_{1} = \left. \frac {\partial \log p} {\partial \log n}\right|_{s} = 1 + \frac {n} {T} \frac {d T} {d n} \Bigg |_{s} \tag {8}$$But Equation (3) implies that, when $\mathbf {s} =$ constant,$$c_{v} \frac {d T} {d n} = \frac {p} {n^{2}} = k \frac {T} {n} = \left( c_{p} - c_{v}\right) \frac {T} {n} . \tag {9}$$Substituting Equation (9) in Equation (8) gives us$$\Gamma_{1} = 1 + \frac {\left( \mathrm {c}_{\mathrm {p}} - \mathrm {c}_{\mathrm {v}}\right)} {\mathrm {c}_{\mathrm {v}}} = \gamma .$$
